name: jboss_wildfly
explanation: JBoss/WildFly is a JMX integration handled by JMXFetch. The spec marks `jmx_url` as required (e.g. `service:jmx:remote+http://<HOST>:<PORT>` for WildFly 10+, `service:jmx:http-remoting-jmx://...` for 9 and older), and the spec also mandates a custom client JAR (`custom_jar_paths` pointing at `jboss-cli-client.jar`). Production JBoss/WildFly management interfaces require authentication (a ManagementRealm user provisioned via `add-user.sh`), so `user`/`password` are effectively required. None of these (URL scheme variant, client JAR path, management realm credentials) can be inferred from a wire probe.
notes: Standard JMX-impossible pattern (also see weblogic, tomcat, cassandra), with the additional twist that JBoss requires a vendor-specific client JAR on the Agent host.
